name: Library Inventory
description: Shelving and inventory management for a public library

vocabulary.definition: Scanning every book on a shelf to verify they are in correct Dewey Decimal order and flagging misplaced items
Reviewing the collection to identify and remove old, damaged, or outdated books from circulation
A book reserved for a specific patron; must be pulled from the shelf and placed on the holds shelf for pickup
A book officially removed from the library collection; stamped WITHDRAWN and disposed of per policy
The numbering system used to organize nonfiction books by subject (e.g., 800s = Literature, 600s = Technology)
The small sticker on a book's spine showing its call number, used to determine correct shelf position
The combination of Dewey number and author cutter code that gives a book its unique shelf address
The trolley where checked-in books wait before being sorted and re-shelved

commonAsks: We have a hold that needs pulling before we close.
The nonfiction section looks rough — can you do a shelf-read?
When you get a chance, can you weed the 600s?
Can you check if we have any withdrawn items that need to come off the floor?
